@DarkEndMoon1 @mrddmia @RonColeman I'm not saying that wouldn't be a good rule to have. I am saying that the claim that it's obvious that it's the case seems quite absurd without some citation to some authority holding so.

@DarkEndMoon1 @mrddmia @RonColeman It is not obvious to me that given that Congress has the power to create or destroy courts inferior to the Supreme Court, they nevertheless don't have the power to monitor with little or no restriction what goes on in those inferior courts.

@dogg_not @RAHarrisonPA @RobertFreundLaw @DrSarteschi Also, to address the libel issue more directly, we don't hold people liable for libel absent some evidence of wrongdoing. If, with no wrongdoing, I libel you and cause you severe damages, you still have no remedy at law in the United States.

Why do we do this? Because we don't
@mrddmia @RonColeman Do you have any citation to precedential holdings that make it obvious that they couldn't do so even if a statute says they can? It's certainly not obvious to me.

probability.last_reasoning: Bear case increased from 10% to 12%: extreme fear (F&G: 10), compound stress deteriorating toward two-leg ELEVATED, Brent approaching $85, USD/JPY firmly above 157, and Hormuz escalation risk. Base case slightly increased from 40% to 42%: despite positive regulatory momentum (Trump/CFTC/Hill), the macro headwinds, extreme fear, and XRP at $1.36 suggest the market is pricing in near-term downside risk that could keep price suppressed even if fundamentals improve. Mid case reduced from 38% to 35%: the macro deterioration and compound stress pre-loading reduce the probability of a smooth institutional adoption ramp in 2026. Bull case reduced from 12% to 11%: escalating geopolitical risk makes the high-end scenario marginally less likely in the near term, despite regulatory tailwinds.

bear_case.score_reasoning: Bear pressure elevated from macro deterioration (compound stress approaching ELEVATED, extreme fear, DXY surge) and persistent data gaps (ODL volume, permissioned DEX adoption). However, the regulatory counter-argument has weakened significantly with direct presidential intervention. The strongest bear argument is now macro-driven rather than thesis-fundamental.

competing_infrastructure.name: SWIFT GPI
competing_infrastructure.status_text: SWIFT continues upgrading infrastructure but no new announcements countering XRPL corridors. Trump's push for crypto market structure implicitly deprioritizes incumbent rails.

competing_infrastructure.name: Visa B2B Connect
competing_infrastructure.status_text: No new competitive signals. Visa B2B Connect remains focused on traditional card-adjacent rails rather than institutional settlement.

competing_infrastructure.name: JPMorgan Kinexys
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Kinexys remains the most credible direct competitor for institutional settlement. No new expansion announcements but JPMorgan's banking lobby against Clarity Act suggests they view crypto rails as competitive threat.

competing_infrastructure.name: BIS Project Nexus
competing_infrastructure.status_text: No new developments. The sovereign settlement alternative remains the primary structural threat to the thesis — central banks preferring government-controlled infrastructure over private token settlement.

competing_infrastructure.name: Ethereum Institutional
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Ondo Finance actively building tokenized stock infrastructure on Ethereum with NYSE/Nasdaq liquidity inheritance. This is a direct competitor for RWA tokenization market share that XRPL is also targeting.

bear_case.odl_stagnation: ODL volume data remains completely opaque — now the longest sustained data gap in the thesis framework. Ripple's unified Payments announcement included no volume metrics, which could indicate either strategic timing or disappointing numbers.
bear_case.token_velocity_concern: Token velocity data remains unknown. XRP 24h volume at $3.0B against $83B market cap suggests ~3.6% daily turnover, consistent with speculative trading rather than utility settlement demand.

macro_headwinds.name: Global Recession Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: S&P 500 at 6,816 shows equity resilience but F&G at 10 and compound stress approaching ELEVATED suggest risk-off rotation is accelerating in crypto/risk assets.

macro_headwinds.name: Crypto Winter Signals
macro_headwinds.status_text: F&G at 10 is extreme fear territory. XRP down from cycle highs with persistent selling pressure. If macro stress triggers ELEVATED compound status, crypto could see forced liquidation cascades.

macro_headwinds.name: Regulatory Reversal Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: Materially reduced by Trump's direct intervention, CFTC Chair's public statements, and House Financial Committee engagement. The political alignment on crypto regulation is the strongest it has been.

macro_headwinds.name: Rate Hike Extension
macro_headwinds.status_text: US 10Y at 4.05% stable but BOJ trapped in tightening trajectory. If BOJ hikes to defend yen, global rate differential dynamics shift and could extend higher-for-longer environment.

bear_case.bear_narrative: HOLD with increased monitoring. The thesis stands on two legs — one strong, one invisible. The strong leg: 7 ETF issuers committed $1.4B+ through fiduciary process, and the Clarity Act has the most favorable legislative configuration observed (POTUS, CFTC Chair, House Financial Committee all pushing). The invisible leg: ODL settlement volume, the thesis's most critical metric, remains completely unverifiable at T-10 months from its kill switch deadline, and Ripple's post-SEC silence is now classified as SUSPICIOUS_ABSENCE. The paradox persists: institutional capital is flowing in while Ripple's own behavior (RLUSD 77% on Ethereum, rebrand away from ODL, multi-rail messaging) suggests the company is hedging away from XRP-as-settlement-layer toward XRP-as-one-option. No kill switch is triggered, but two are now at WARNING. The question the system cannot answer — is XRP actually settling institutional volume at scale — is the only question that ultimately matters.
